dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4853 IS: United States-Israel Agriculture Cooperation Improvement and Expansion Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Hoeven (for himself and Mrs. Gillibrand) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations

form.official-title: To amend the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 to modify the BARD Fund, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the United States-Israel Agriculture Cooperation Improvement and Expansion Act.

paragraph.text: the United States-Israel Binational Agricultural Research and Development Fund (referred to in this section as the BARD Fund)—

subparagraph.text: is a competitive funding program that supports collaborative agricultural research in areas of mutual interest to the United States and Israel;

paragraph.text: the primary mission of the BARD Fund is to bring together United States and Israeli scientists to jointly address key agricultural and food production challenges that concern both countries;
since its establishment, the BARD Fund has approved more than 1,300 research projects, with a total investment of $315,000,000 that has gained $16 for every $1 spent; and

research funded by the BARD Fund has led to new agricultural practices, commercial engagements, patents, and breeding rights licenses, which serve as a growth engine to the United States and Israeli economies.

section.text: Section 1458(e) of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 (7 U.S.C. 3291(e)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (1), by striking entered into and inserting as entered into in 1977;

subparagraph.text: in subparagraph (A), by striking and at the end;
in subparagraph (B), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:
support mid-stage research to advance cooperative agricultural research projects of mutual interest to the United States and Israel.

paragraph.text: by adding at the end the following:
The BARD Fund shall establish an accelerator program that supports mid-stage research, as determined by the technology readiness level, in priority areas established by the BARD Fund, and—

subparagraph.text: fast-tracks cooperative research between scientists participating in BARD Fund activities;
accelerates the successful development of agricultural research through resources and services developed or orchestrated by the BARD Fund;
provides management guidance, technical assistance, and consulting to scientists participating in activities described in paragraph (2); or
advances cooperative agricultural research projects of mutual interest to the United States and Israel.

paragraph.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the BARD Fund to carry out activities under this subsection $8,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2027 through 2031.
